_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Lung Abscess/ : Lung Abscess_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Lung Abscess_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: as a complication of aspiration pneumonia caused by mouth anaerobes.
Patients with Periodontal disease, Seizure disorder, Alcohol abuse, Dysphagia

preauth.label: Clinical notes detailing examination findings, previous surgery/procedure, follow-up visit details, investigations, advise for daycare procedure
Chest X Ray and CT scan Chest (as applicable)
CBC, Blood Sugar (Fasting, PP and Random)

claim.label: Post treatment Chest X ray submitted?
Post treatment CBC, Blood Sugar (Fasting, PP and Random) reports

ppd_preauth.q: Is there Localized collection of pus in the lungs in one or more cavities on chest X ray report show presence of Abscess?
Are the Sputum smear and culture reports available?

common_queries: Localized collection of pus in the lungs in one or more cavities is not shown on chest X-ray report or CT scan, indicating absence of Abscess.
Sputum smear and culture reports are not available.

Mandatory pre-authorization document 'Clinical notes detailing examination findings, previous surgery/procedure, follow-up visit details, investigations, or advise for daycare procedure' is missing or incomplete.
Post-treatment Chest X-ray report not submitted at claim stage.
Detailed discharge summary missing at claim submission.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/DNB/ equivalent (in General Medicine / Pediatric Medicine)
clinical_key_pointers: is defined as necrosis of the pulmonary tissue and formation of cavities containing necrotic debris or fluid caused by microbial infection.
A patient with an ongoing pneumonia of 2 to 3 weeks duration
History of pneumonia in recent past with respiratory symptoms
Cough, sputum that may be putrid and foul smelling
Pleuritic chest pain or heaviness in the chest
